results.title: The American republic
results.creator_credit: Art Cohen, Metropolitan Museum of Art and WGBH Educational Foundation
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “The Western tradition”.
This absorbing series is a survey of 2,000 years of Western civilisation illustrated by paintings, sculpture and other records from the past, compiled by the Metropolitan Museum of Art. Historian Eugen Weber analyses the American Republic which was comprised of radicals and conservatives and founded on the premises of “Universal Freedoms” for its inhabitants.

results.title: The Amazon: life along the river in Peru
results.brief_description: Following the Amazon River to its sources in the High Andes of Peru, this film surveys the geography of the Maranon river area and the peoples way of life and variation in climate and vegetation on the western and eastern slopes and in the foothills of the Andes. Also shown is the city of Iquitos - the trade centre of the Upper Andes and its environs, the abundantly productive rainforests No other credits available.

results.title: The Amazing laser
results.creator_credit: Eddie Newstead, Richard Reisz and London Television Service
results.brief_description: The laser has applications in many fields, from entertainment to scientific research. Looks at some of the ways in which lasers are being used, and possible future development with lasers. It is being used in the printing industry for etching copper plates, in the search for cheap energy by producing fusion reactions in the laboratory, as a scalpel in surgery, and for making holograms. Narrated by Paul Vaughan.

results.title: The Alternatives to Infertility
results.brief_description: In this program, the various methods of treatment that are available to infertile couples are listed. The aim of the program is to teach both patients and the community at large about current reproductive technology. The issues of ethics, morality and legality are not discussed.

results.title: The Allport collection
results.brief_description: The Allport Collection is a unique assembly of the art of early Tasmania and various objects of the 19th Century, housed in the State Library of Tasmania. In this documentary the curator of the collection gives some insight into its purpose and historical importance. No other credits available.

results.title: The Aims of irrigation
results.brief_description: The film outlines briefly the three aims of controlled irrigation: to supply the water requirements of the growing crop; to use water efficiently; to avoid soil damage. It stresses that the achievement of these amis should be the constant endeavour of every irrigation farmer so as to maintain soil fertility, and to obtain the maximum return for their labour.

results.title: The Age of revolutions: W. Blake, poet and painter
results.creator_credit: Edward Hayward, BBC-TV and Open University
results.brief_description: Relates two sides of Blake’s work which are normally considered separately - his art, particularly his illustrations, and his poetry. Concentrates on “Songs of Innocence and Experience” and the much closer relationship between text and illustrations than usually admitted. The delicate colours of the illustrations, often no more than half an inch across, are shown in close-up.

results.title: The African scene: youth builds a nation in Tanzania
results.creator_credit: H Lemieux, Encyclopaedia Britannica Films and Visual Education Center, Toronto
results.brief_description: Emphasises the importance of “nation-building” by showing girls of a government-operated secondary school in Tanzania helping their country to develop a better standard of living. Also shows use of co-operative farming as a means of increasing agricultural production and improving rural life.
results.creator_credit_for_label: H Lemieux, Encyclopaedia Britannica Films and Visual Education Center, Toronto

results.title: The Affair-Quandary
results.brief_description: A woman is in a quandary about whether to confront her husband with her suspicions that he is having an affair. Her husband’s quandary is whether to tell his wife, leave his wife or break off the affair. This program will interest people in relationships, educators, counsellors, community health and women’s organisations and marriage counselling services.

results.title: The Adze and the chainsaw
results.creator_credit: Pat McGuire, Tom Williamson and New Zealand National Film Unit
results.brief_description: Explores the question of whether Maori art should retain its traditional forms, or reflect changing experiences and new influences. Sequences include students making murals combining Maori legends with modern day experiences and the restoration of carvings using new techniques under the supervision of a traditional Maori artist. Suitable for upper secondary and tertiary levels. Camera by Bayly Watson, edited by Ross Chambers, researched by Judith Wright.
